meta.descriptor: PARIS, FRANCE → SYDNEY, NSW · 51–200 · AI OUTBOUND PLATFORM

cover.kicker: MARKET ENTRY REPORT · FRENCH SAAS ENTERING AUSTRALIA
cover.headline: Australia's AI-sales market is growing 18.5% a year — and none of the incumbents are local.
cover.scope: lemlist's AI-powered outbound platform — lemlist and lemAgent — helps 20,000+ sales teams find leads, detect buying intent, and run personalised multichannel campaigns while actively avoiding spam.

narrative: Australia's AI sales tools segment is valued at **USD 2.5 billion (2024)**, forecast to USD 10.8B by 2033 at **18.5% CAGR** {chip:est}; marketing automation — lemlist's core territory — is the fastest-expanding sub-segment at 14.80% {chip:sourced}. **NSW attracted AUD 1.7B across 160 deals in 2025**, with AI-native SaaS the dominant theme {chip:sourced}.

Australian buyers prioritise **human-approved AI outreach, local data residency, and APAC timezone support**. The R&DTI offers a **43.5% refundable offset** (under AUD 20M turnover) on AI development conducted locally; **EMDG** can reimburse up to 50% of eligible export marketing spend {chip:sourced}.
exec.keyQuestionAnswer: NSW is deploying capital into AI-native SaaS right now — AI drove **61% of national startup funding** {chip:sourced}. Most relevant at your stage: Blackbird, AirTree, Tidal, OIF and Antler (AU$250K–10M tickets); corporate venture arms (Telstra, Westpac, CBA) co-invest alongside pilots. Key highlights: [Cheryl Mack](/mentors/experts/cheryl-mack) (CEO, Aussie Angels) and [1V (OneVentures)](/investors/onevntures) (Sydney VC, $1B+, Series B+ — your stage).

highlights.name: Cheryl Mack
highlights.why: Directly connects founders to Australia's largest active angel network

highlights.name: 1V (OneVentures)
highlights.why: $1B+ Sydney VC; high-growth scale-ups at lemlist's stage

heroStat.label: AI SHARE OF AU STARTUP FUNDING, 2025
heroStat.caption: AI drove 61% of total national startup funding — the raise window is open now.

sequence.label: THE REPORT'S ENTRY SEQUENCE

rows.text: Local entity + APAC support capability; R&DTI registration; Spam Act audit
Two to three AU reference customers in SaaS or BFSI; pilot-attached corporate venture conversations
Sydney traction anchors the next round; national expansion

sequence.caveat: A suggested shape, not a prescription — sequencing is the core agenda for your strategy session.

tiles.caption: Australia AI sales tools, 2024 — → USD 10.8B by 2033
CAGR, marketing automation — lemlist's core sub-segment
Marketing-automation share of CRM marketing services, 2025
NSW startup funding 2025 across 160 deals — AI-native SaaS dominant

metrics.footnote: EST = model-derived estimate from the available research.

strengths.text: Automated prospect research + intent-based outreach match the AI-first workflow shift
51–200 staff; enterprise segment holds 68.43% of CRM marketing services spend
France–Australia corridor + EU-Australia FTA ease cross-border SaaS
80% of B2B interactions projected digital by 2026 — tools that cut noise win

weaknesses.text: Growth-stage burn AUD $120–250k/mo; senior AI engineers to $180k+
75% of vendors moving off per-seat toward usage/outcome pricing — mid-shift entry
Stack-anchor incumbents (Salesforce, HubSpot) demand brand-authority investment

opportunities.text: $1.2B AI-focused VC in Sydney in 18 months {chip:sourced}
SME automation adoption growing 16.21% CAGR — the self-serve fit
43.5% refundable offset on AU-conducted AI development

threats.text: Mandatory AI disclosure by Dec 2026; penalties to $50M {chip:sourced}
ACMA aggressively enforces inferred-consent rules; unsubscribes within 5 working days
61% of B2B buyers prefer rep-free — outbound volume alone won't land

competitors.intro: The AU sales-tech landscape is shifting to agentic workflows, yet remains dominated by legacy US platforms — and none of the surfaced competitors shows a dedicated Australian footprint. That absence of localised presence IS the gap.

you.name: lemlist
you.position: AI outbound platform — lead discovery, intent signals, multichannel sequencing, spam avoidance
you.strengths: AI agents research and engage on autopilot; deliverability moat
you.differs: EXECUTION LAYER, NOT JUST DATA — AI AGENT AUTOPILOT

rows.name: Apollo.io
rows.position: Lead discovery + outbound in one; high value-to-cost for SMBs
rows.differs: Data quality inconsistent; less agentic research depth

rows.name: Salesloft
rows.strengths: Deep enterprise CRM integration; AI deal-risk management
rows.differs: Heavy and pricey for agile growth-stage teams

rows.name: ZoomInfo
rows.position: Global leader in enterprise GTM intelligence
rows.differs: Expensive, long onboarding; overkill for outbound automation

rows.name: Lusha
rows.differs: Data provider — no multichannel sequencing or agents

rows.name: Cognism
rows.strengths: Phone-verified data — mobile outreach matters in AU

rows.name: Agentforce Sales
rows.position: AI sales automation inside the Salesforce ecosystem
rows.strengths: Seamless CRM integration; predictive insights from customer data
rows.differs: Restrictive outside Salesforce; no specialised outbound

competitors.gaps: **1 · No localised presence** among any major global competitor — a Sydney footprint is itself a moat. **2 · Data vs execution** — most rivals sell data for humans; agentic execution is uncontested. **3 · Deliverability** — spam avoidance is a technical moat generic sequencers lack.
competitors.positioningRead: Position as the **AI Outbound Partner for the Australian SaaS Ecosystem** — pitch 'Automated Personalisation at Scale' (the fastest-growing CRM application, 18.49% CAGR) to move from tool to revenue engine. Note: the matcher also surfaced two low-relevance profiles (an entertainer's personal site and an inbound-only tool) — excluded here by the relevance gate (R11).
competitors.scanHookCopy: We can profile adjacent players — Outreach, Instantly, Smartlead, Reply.io — on request.

accounts.intro: No target companies were named in your intake, so no account briefs could be built — the ICP profile below is the research's read on where to aim first.

icpGuidance.sectorFocus: Australian B2B SaaS companies expanding their GTM teams (1–3 month buying cycles, lightweight RFP) — then BFSI and retail/e-commerce enterprise (6–12 month cycles, data-residency proof required).
icpGuidance.angle: Position lemlist as the efficiency layer for CRM marketing services (24.18% market share) — automated personalisation that solves the sales-talent shortage.

accounts.worthKnowing: Australian buyers favour fixed-price pilots with milestone definitions over time-and-materials, and regulated-sector buyers require human-approval loops in AI outreach under Spam Act scrutiny.

providers.intro: Given your stated priority — the raise — the three below map most directly to investor readiness. All six are credible matches.

ourRead.name: Fullstack Advisory
ourRead.why: Capital-raise support, financial modelling, and R&DTI claims — register AI/ML development within ten months of financial year-end to preserve eligibility.

ourRead.name: oSpace (NEXTGEN)
ourRead.why: A local sales footprint — prospecting and intent-data analysis — without immediate office overhead; the bridge while the Sydney entity stands up.

ourRead.name: ADAPT
ourRead.why: Private roundtables with CIO/CMO buyers in BFSI and retail — the two largest CRM marketing services segments.

all.name: NEXTGEN Group
all.description: Sales-as-a-service and reseller network across ANZ enterprise accounts.

all.name: Stone & Chalk
all.description: Australia's largest innovation community; private suites ~$3–6k/mo; resident corporate venture arms (Westpac, CBA).

all.name: Accenture Australia
all.description: Integration partner — CRM implementation holds 32.41% of the CRM marketing services market.

gov.name: Business France
gov.why: Tailored support, market introductions and investment guidance for French entrants — the corridor body for this entry

gov.name: AiGroup
gov.why: Industry advocacy + employer compliance guidance (super, Fair Work, bargaining)

gov.name: Export Connect
gov.why: B2B lead qualification and export business-development strategy

hubs.name: Stone & Chalk
hubs.description: Premier venue for mentors and investment access; bank CVC partners in residence.

hubs.name: H2 Ventures
hubs.description: AI and data specialist; seed funding and regulatory guidance.

hubs.name: UNSW Founders 10x
hubs.description: Capital-raise support and mentorship via SAFE notes; founder-to-founder VC credibility.

hubs.name: SproutX
hubs.description: National accelerator; commercialisation model and industry mentors.

mentors.intro: Mentors who understand the local SaaS landscape accelerate fundraising — these five offer pathways to capital and enterprise sales expertise.

primary.name: Cheryl Mack
primary.why: Access to angel syndicates and VCs — Australia's largest active angel network; flagged in your key-question answer.

primary.name: Daniel Grindrod
primary.why: Outbound strategy refinement; experience helping European founders with APAC expansion.

primary.name: Navaid Khatib
primary.why: Cross-border product management; local regulatory and payments landscape.

extra.name: Dónal Brady
extra.why: Scaling platforms across APAC with operational discipline.

extra.name: Ben Morrisroe
extra.why: Relocating a tech business to Australia — infrastructure perspective.

investors.intro: The research suggests targeting institutional VCs for growth-stage depth plus strategic angels for local networks — the order below is one possible read; which doors first is the strategy-session conversation.

approachOrder.label: Institutional depth first
approachOrder.text: [1V (OneVentures)](/investors/onevntures) ($1B+, Series B+ — the stage match) and [Grok Ventures](/investors/grok-ventures) for capital depth and APAC credibility.

approachOrder.label: AI-thesis validation
approachOrder.text: [77 Partners](/investors/77-partners) (AI & Data focus, AU$100k min) to validate PMF within the AU AI sales vertical.

approachOrder.label: Strategic angels
approachOrder.text: [Stephane Chatonsky](/investors/stephane-chatonsky) ($50–100k; 25 yrs PE, SaaS) bridging French origins and the AU landscape; [Mick Liubinskas](/investors/mick-liubinskas) ($25k; serial operator) for local operational depth.

all.name: 1V (OneVentures)
all.description: Sydney; $1B+ under management; high-growth scale-ups — the primary stage match.

all.name: 77 Partners
all.description: Brisbane; AI & Data thesis — high-signal for the core platform.

all.name: Stephane Chatonsky
all.description: 25+ years PE; SaaS focus; mentor-investor for the expansion.

all.name: Mick Liubinskas
all.description: Sydney serial founder-operator; SaaS and professional services.

investors.alsoNamed: Also active per the research: **Blackbird, AirTree, Tidal, OIF, Antler Australia** (AU$250K–10M AI/SaaS tickets) and corporate venture arms of **Telstra, Westpac, CBA** — pilot-attached capital.

cards.name: Venture & Capital 2026
cards.why: Concentrated HNW and institutional investors — a growth-stage company with revenue stands out among earlier-stage pitches.

cards.name: Startup To Scaleup Summit
cards.why: Dual-track: enterprise clients for PMF validation + pitch sessions drawing angel syndicates and venture scouts.

cards.name: Venture Capital World Summit
cards.why: Global and local VCs — the room to frame AI outbound against AU productivity challenges.

maximise.lead: Pre-event outreach with your own product
maximise.text: Run a lemlist multichannel campaign targeting speakers and registered investors of Venture & Capital 2026 two weeks prior — the product demo IS the outreach.
Australian investors are risk-averse — bring AU pilot results or case studies to the Scaleup Summit.
Position European origins as strength in data privacy and AI ethics — increasingly weighted by AU institutional investors.

actionPlan.intro: Three phases over six months — the default map; pace set together in the strategy session.

groups.title: Legal & structural
bullets: Pty Ltd via ASIC (~$310/yr)
ABN + GST over $75k
[Fullstack Advisory](/service-providers/fullstack-advisory) registers AI/ML under R&DTI — 43.5% refundable, up to $4M/yr cash.

groups.title: Compliance
bullets: Audit AI features against the Privacy Act; ADM disclosures before Dec 2026
Spam Act: inferred consent, 5-day unsubscribes, sender ID
DNCR checks for voice.

groups.title: Investor groundwork
bullets: Map Series A/B profile against Blackbird, AirTree, Tidal, Antler
[Business France](/government-support/business-france) warm introductions
apply to [H2 Ventures](/innovation-ecosystem/h2-ventures) + [SproutX](/innovation-ecosystem/sproutx).

groups.title: Presence
bullets: Base at [Stone & Chalk](/service-providers/stone-and-chalk) ($3–6k/mo suites; Westpac + CBA CVC in residence)
budget $80–150k/mo burn for a 3–5 person team ($100–180k salaries + 12% super).

groups.title: Team & partners
bullets: Hire an AI-fluent Sydney Head of Sales (national quota attainment: 46% — favour RevOps/GTM-engineer backgrounds)
[ADAPT](/service-providers/adapt) CIO/CMO community
[AiGroup](/government-support/aigroup) employer compliance.

groups.title: Investor momentum
bullets: [UNSW Founders 10x](/innovation-ecosystem/unsw-founders-10x) events for founder-to-founder VC credibility
pursue CVC pilots via Stone & Chalk's bank partners.

groups.title: Sales motion
bullets: B2B SaaS heads of sales first (1–3 month cycles)
fixed-price milestone pilots
human-approval loops in all AI outreach.

groups.title: Channels
bullets: [NEXTGEN Group](/service-providers/nextgen-group) reseller network
[Export Connect](/government-support/export-connect) lead qualification
[Accenture Australia](/service-providers/accenture-australia) as SI partner.

groups.title: Pricing & EMDG
bullets: Pilot usage/outcome pricing alongside seats (75% of vendors are shifting)
localise case studies before enterprise procurement
submit EMDG for up to 50% of eligible launch marketing spend.

compliance.intro: The Australia–France Tax Treaty (2006) and the concluded EU-Australia FTA smooth the corridor; the structural requirements are the same gate every foreign entrant passes.

table.finding: At least one director ordinarily resident in Australia; Director ID for non-residents takes 4–6 weeks (paper process)
table.note: Start first; local hire or nominee director

table.finding: GST over A$75k (10%); ABN 1–14 days, TFN up to 28 days
table.note: France treaty reduces withholding on dividends/royalties; R&DTI 43.5% under A$20M turnover

table.finding: Aggressive enforcement; inferred-consent rules; unsubscribe within 5 working days
table.note: Existential for an outbound platform — audit every sequence before launch; embed human approval

table.finding: 482 (up to 4 yrs) / 400 (short-term setup) visas; 12% super; modern awards set pay scales
table.note: Airwallex / Wise Business interim banking while overseas-director verification clears

checklist.text: AI lead-scoring/outreach transparency before Dec 2026; penalties to $50M
Every automated sequence; DNCR for voice
A stated enterprise buyer filter — address in GTM positioning
Within ten months of financial year-end
Up to 50% of eligible export marketing spend

guides.intro: Each answers a question this report raises — enterprise AI positioning, partner-led growth, data-residency expectations.

cards.title: How Anthropic entered the Australian market
cards.summary: Relationship-first strategies and cloud partnerships behind enterprise wins (Canva, CBA).
cards.relevantBecause: The playbook for AI credibility with AU enterprise buyers.

cards.title: How UiPath entered the Australian market
cards.summary: Long-term partnerships to scale agentic AI locally.
cards.relevantBecause: The closest automation precedent to lemAgent.

cards.title: How Shopify won Australian merchants with partner-led growth
cards.summary: Penetration through ecosystem partners over direct sales.
cards.relevantBecause: Maps to the NEXTGEN/Accenture channel strategy in §10.

cards.title: How GitHub entered the Australian market
cards.summary: Data residency and local engineering support in enterprise AI sales.
cards.relevantBecause: Data residency is a stated AU buyer filter for lemlist.

cards.title: How Zoho played the long game in Australia with local data centres
cards.relevantBecause: The narrative for security-conscious enterprise procurement.

leads.gapCopy: No matching pre-built lead datasets for your industry exist in the directory today.
leads.customBuildCopy: A bespoke build can target: Australian Series B+ VCs · B2B SaaS companies expanding GTM teams · BFSI and retail/e-commerce marketing-automation buyers.

close.headline: This report is the map. The route gets chosen together.
close.body: What it deliberately doesn't do is lock in strategy: sequencing the raise against reference-customer wins, pilot pricing, and which investors to approach first are worked through in your included 60-minute strategy session.
arriveWith: Raise first vs land two reference customers first
Mid-market self-serve vs BFSI enterprise as the beachhead
Per-seat vs usage-based pricing for the AU market
Which two funds and which angel to approach first

analyst: Mordor Intelligence — AU digital-marketing + CRM services sizing
vendor: Lusha, Salesloft, Apollo.io, ZoomInfo, Cognism, Agentforce product materials
